refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target throughout the operational period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (link availability) and ties it explicitly to the customer-defined target and operational timeframe, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime and downtime over the evaluation period using telemetry logs and compare against the customer-defined availability threshold.
sub_frs: FR1: Ensure continuous network link connectivity for teleoperation.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Monitor and report link status to relevant modules.

candidate_dps: DP1: Redundant network link architecture with failover capability.
DP2: Real-time link health monitoring and automatic reconnection protocols.
DP3: Telemetry data aggregation and status reporting subsystem.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Redundant network link architecture with failover capability.

potential_sources: Link monitoring tightly coupled with failover control logic.
Telemetry reporting depends on link status data.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically supports FR1 and partially FR2; coupling with monitoring and reporting DPs is expected but manageable.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network infrastructure supports redundancy and failover.
Risk of increased system complexity impacting maintainability.
Risk that failover mechanisms may introduce brief link interruptions.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 and DP3 in subsequent decomposition levels to address monitoring and reporting explicitly.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Implements network link redundancy and failover mechanisms for teleoperation link availability.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connections managed for redundancy and failover.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends link status and availability metrics to monitoring and reporting modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands contingent on link availability.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of link availability logic; low diffusion as failover is centralized.
Vehicle module depends on link status but does not contain failover logic.
Receives safety alerts related to link failures but does not implement link availability.
Overall system boundary; contains coordination but not detailed link failover.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing link availability and failover logic within the Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential single point of failure if Teleoperation module is compromised.
Integration complexity with Vehicle and Safety Services modules for status propagation.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and fallback behaviors with Vehicle and Safety Services to mitigate integration risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (redundant network link with failover) directly addresses the refined FR sub-requirements and is logically sound.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as failover logic is centralized in Teleoperation module.
Need to monitor integration points with Vehicle and Safety Services for status updates.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best ownership candidate for the DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and link availability logs is feasible within the Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of link availability responsibility to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Further decompose FR2 and FR3 to explicitly address monitoring and reporting in next level.
Clarify interface protocols between Teleoperation, Vehicle, and Safety Services.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition and assignments are sound, further refinement of monitoring and reporting sub-FRs and their corresponding DPs and IMs is needed to fully address the requirement.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Ensure continuous network link connectivity for teleoperation.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Monitor and report link status to relevant modules.

revise.why: To explicitly decompose monitoring and reporting functions for clearer DP and IM assignments in subsequent levels.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports redundancy and failover.
Telemetry interfaces provide accurate and timely link status.
open_risks: Integration complexity with Vehicle and Safety Services modules.
Potential brief link interruptions during failover.
evidence_needed: Telemetry logs demonstrating link availability ≥ 0.999 over evaluation period.
Test results of failover mechanisms under simulated link failure conditions.
